quick_facts.label: What this node represents
quick_facts.value: “State Forest Service Examination” is best treated as an umbrella for state-administered forest-recruitment examinations, not one all-India examination: Uttarakhand’s 2025 notice uses “Assistant Conservator of Forest, Logging Officer and Forest Range Officer Combined Examination,” while Telangana has separately notified Forest Range Officer recruitment.

quick_facts.label: Typical entry post
quick_facts.value: A principal direct-recruitment outcome is Forest Range Officer / Forest Range Officer Grade-I; Rajasthan’s service rules provide for 50% direct recruitment and 50% promotion for Forest Range Officer Grade-I.

quick_facts.label: Forestry degree relevance
quick_facts.value: A B.Sc. (Hons.) Forestry is directly relevant to eligibility examples: Rajasthan’s rules name a bachelor’s degree in Forestry, and Telangana’s Forest Range Officer notice lists Forestry among acceptable subjects.

quick_facts.label: Controlling document
quick_facts.value: The particular state commission’s current advertisement is controlling for vacancies, dates, age limits, reservation, fees, physical standards, syllabus, and the selection stages; candidates should not transfer details from another state’s notice.

sections.heading: Purpose and scope
paragraphs: This node covers competitive state recruitment routes into state forest departments, especially Range-level posts such as Forest Range Officer (FRO). It should not be confused with the Union Public Service Commission’s Indian Forest Service examination: the responsible recruiting authority, examination title, post mix, and rules are determined by the relevant State Public Service Commission or state recruiting body. For example, Uttarakhand combined ACF, Logging Officer, and FRO posts in one 2025 examination, whereas Telangana issued a Forest Range Officer recruitment notification.

A Range Forest Officer is a field-management role within the forest-department structure. Maharashtra’s Forest Department describes forest divisions as being divided into range units headed by a Range Forest Officer; Rajasthan’s forest records also show operational responsibilities involving protection, patrol/monitoring, and departmental management at range level.
bullets: Use the examination to seek appointment in the state named in the notification; it is not a transferable national qualification.
Read the advertised post name carefully: a notice may recruit FRO alone or combine it with Assistant Conservator of Forests, Logging Officer, or other forest-service posts.

sections.heading: Eligibility: degree, age, and physical fitness
paragraphs: A forestry honours degree is a strong academic route, but eligibility remains state- and notification-specific. Rajasthan’s Forest Subordinate Service Rules list a bachelor’s degree in Forestry among qualifications for direct recruitment to Forest Range Officer Grade-I; the same rules also accept specified science subjects, Agriculture, and Engineering. Telangana’s FRO notice similarly includes Forestry among a wider list of accepted disciplines.

Do not assume that academic eligibility alone is sufficient. State notices can set an age window, category-based relaxations, physical measurements, endurance/walking tests, and medical-fitness requirements. In the Telangana notice located for this research, the stated age range and physical standards were specific to that recruitment cycle, so they are historical examples rather than current universal requirements.
bullets: Before applying, match your degree title and subjects against the exact notice—not merely this node or another state’s rules.
Check the notice’s cut-off date for age and qualification, domicile/language requirements where applicable, reservation documentation, and physical/medical standards.
Obtain prescribed certificates only in the format and from the authority named in the advertisement.

sections.heading: Selection format varies by state
paragraphs: There is no single standard pattern. Uttarakhand’s Assistant Conservator of Forest, Logging Officer and Forest Range Officer Combined Examination–2025 illustrates a multi-stage design: a preliminary objective paper, a written main examination with compulsory and optional papers, and an interview. Its preliminary paper was General Studies and General Aptitude; its mains scheme included General Hindi, General English, General Knowledge, two optional subjects, and an interview.

Other state notices may organize selection differently and may separately assess physical standards or fitness. Treat a state’s current notification, syllabus, and instructions as the authoritative pattern for that cycle.
bullets: Download the official syllabus and scheme before planning optional-subject preparation.
Confirm whether the preliminary stage is screening-only or counted in final merit, and whether any paper has a minimum qualifying mark.
Check the notice for document verification, physical measurement/endurance testing, medical examination, and interview requirements.

sections.heading: Application process
paragraphs: Apply only through the recruiting commission’s official process during the window in its current advertisement. The UKPSC candidate portal, for example, separately maintains recruitment notifications, admit cards, answer keys, results, syllabi, previous question papers, and an examination calendar—useful checkpoints through the recruitment lifecycle.

Because deadlines, application fees, correction windows, upload specifications, and required certificates are cycle-specific, they should be treated as dated notice information rather than standing facts. Preserve the submitted form, payment/fee record where applicable, photo-ID details, and every notice issued after submission.
bullets: Locate the current advertisement on the relevant state commission’s official recruitment-notifications page.
Read the eligibility and instructions before paying or submitting the form.
Monitor the same official portal for correction notices, admit-card release, answer keys, objections, document verification, physical tests, and results.

sections.heading: Preparation approach
paragraphs: Build preparation from the official scheme rather than from generic “forest service” coaching material. The Uttarakhand 2025 combined examination example required general studies/aptitude at preliminary stage and language, general knowledge, and optional-subject performance in mains; that structure supports a plan combining general competitive-exam skills with disciplined revision of the notified forestry/science optional subjects.

Use official previous question papers and syllabus pages when the commission provides them. UKPSC places both syllabus and old question papers in its Candidate Corner, which makes them more reliable starting points than unofficial reconstructed patterns.
bullets: Create a topic-by-topic checklist from the current official syllabus and map each topic to past official papers where available.
For B.Sc. Forestry graduates, revise core degree material only to the extent it matches the notified optional subjects and syllabus.
Practise timed objective tests, descriptive answers where mains papers require them, and the commission’s stated language-paper requirements.
Prepare physical fitness progressively if the notification requires a walking/endurance test; follow the notified standards and medical instructions rather than informal advice.

sections.heading: Career outcomes after selection
paragraphs: Successful candidates may enter a state forest department in the post named in the recruitment notice, commonly Forest Range Officer or Forest Range Officer Grade-I. This is a state-government career path and service conditions are governed by the respective state’s service rules and appointment orders. Rajasthan’s rules explicitly provide both direct-recruitment and promotional channels for FRO Grade-I, demonstrating that direct recruits join a cadre that also has an internal promotion route.

Range-level work is operational and field-facing: official forest-department material describes ranges as units headed by a Range Forest Officer, while forest codes and conservation plans show range-level responsibilities involving inspection, protection, patrol/monitoring, reporting, and supervision. Exact duties, postings, training, progression, and pay are state- and cadre-specific; salary should be verified from the applicable, dated notification or appointment/service rules.
bullets: Potential appointment title: Forest Range Officer / Forest Range Officer Grade-I, subject to the particular state notice.
Potential work setting: a forest range within a state forest department, with field and administrative responsibilities.
Post-selection requirements may include departmental or in-service training; Rajasthan’s department identifies refresher/orientation training for Range Officers among its HRD activities.
